target.body:
## Summary

- Hardens the plugin tools MCP bridge so unreadable or unwrappable plugin tool descriptors are skipped during handler construction instead of crashing `listTools`.
- Keeps healthy sibling plugin tools listed and callable through the MCP bridge, including the existing `before_tool_call` report-mode wrapper behavior.
- Adds focused regression coverage for throwing `name`, throwing `parameters`, and revoked proxy schema descriptors alongside a healthy sibling tool.
- Out of scope: runtime plugin registry validation and non-MCP provider/tool projection paths.

Transcript note: agent transcript finder returned candidate local sessions; no transcript excerpt is included because approval is required before adding session logs.

## Linked context

Closes: none.
Related: ongoing tool-schema fuzzing hardening workstream.
Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Maintainer fuzzing sweep.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a malformed plugin tool descriptor no longer aborts plugin MCP handler construction or `listTools`; only the malformed descriptor is omitted while valid sibling tools remain exposed and callable.
Real environment tested: local macOS linked gwt worktree rebased onto `origin/main` at `4c32553875b86c27439305c8cc163a03044ecd41`; `origin/main` later advanced to `0a351cdf7f42f03d82643774274b0a26aee66f62` with no changes under `src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts` or `src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.cancel.test.ts src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD -- src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 5m --ttl 20m --timing-json --shell -- "pwd >/dev/null"`.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 files / 10 tests across 2 shards; oxfmt passed; oxlint passed; diff whitespace check passed; final branch autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` with `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.
Observed result after fix: the regression builds MCP handlers from bad descriptors plus `healthy_tool`; `listTools` returns only `healthy_tool`, preserves its input schema, and `callTool` executes it successfully.
What was not tested: live stdio MCP client roundtrip beyond the existing in-process cancellation/list/call coverage; broad changed gate was unavailable.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox is unavailable because `blacksmith testbox list --all` reports `not authenticated`; AWS Crabbox probe is currently blocked by coordinator 401 on run-history/lease creation, so no fresh remote changed gate was available for this PR.
Before evidence: `createPluginToolsMcpHandlers` mapped/wrapped/listed every descriptor with direct `tool.name`, `tool.description`, and `tool.parameters` reads, so one hostile plugin tool descriptor could throw before the MCP bridge listed healthy tools.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.cancel.test.ts src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`

Remote proof attempted:

- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 5m --ttl 20m --timing-json --shell -- "pwd >/dev/null"` -> blocked: coordinator 401 creating run history/lease.

Regression coverage added: `src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts` now covers unreadable plugin tool names, unreadable plugin tool parameters, revoked proxy schemas, and healthy sibling preservation in MCP listing/calling.

What failed before this fix: descriptor wrapping/listing could throw before MCP `listTools` returned, which made one bad plugin tool poison the whole plugin-tools MCP server surface.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes: malformed plugin tools are omitted from the plugin-tools MCP bridge instead of crashing the bridge.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
What is the highest-risk area? Accidentally hiding valid plugin tools while filtering malformed descriptors.
How is that risk mitigated? The filter only drops descriptors whose name, parameters, schema readability, or hook wrapping throws; existing valid listing, calling, approval-report wrapping, and cancellation coverage stayed green.

## Linked context

Closes: none.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? Maintainer fuzzing sweep.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a malformed Ollama tool schema no longer aborts native Ollama request construction before the assistant can produce content; only the bad tool is omitted from the provider request while healthy siblings remain available.
Real environment tested: local macOS linked gwt worktree rebased onto `origin/main` at `20c4e9475a7feab03a5a9653d225ae9973fb1798` plus current remote-runner auth probes.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD -- extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 5m --ttl 20m --timing-json --shell -- "pwd >/dev/null"`.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 1 file / 105 tests; oxfmt passed; oxlint passed; diff whitespace check passed; final branch autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` with `overall: patch is correct (0.82)`.
Observed result after fix: request construction skips tools whose schema cannot be read or safely copied, keeps the healthy `search` sibling in the outgoing Ollama `tools` payload, and preserves large scalar arrays, large property maps, and shared subschema instances that are valid/readable.
What was not tested: live Ollama server execution and non-Ollama provider adapters.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox is unavailable because `blacksmith testbox list --all` reports `not authenticated`; AWS Crabbox probe is currently blocked by coordinator 401 on run-history/lease creation, so no fresh remote changed gate was available for this PR.
Before evidence: native Ollama schema normalization walked `tool.parameters`, nested `properties`, `items`, and union arrays directly with unguarded object/array traversal, so one hostile plugin tool schema could throw during request setup and prevent the assistant turn from reaching provider streaming.

## Tests and validation

Regression coverage added: `extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts` now covers unreadable nested properties, throwing `parameters`, revoked proxies, cycles, shared subschemas, large enum arrays, and wide property maps in native Ollama tool schemas.

What failed before this fix: malformed schema traversal could throw before native Ollama request dispatch, aborting the stream instead of dropping only the bad tool.

## Risk checklist

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): keep the message_sending gate when a channel sets beforeDeliver
target.body:
## Problem

When a channel installs its own dispatcher `beforeDeliver`, the `message_sending` hook is silently skipped for that channel's replies.

In both `dispatchInboundMessageWithBufferedDispatcher` and `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher`, a channel-supplied `beforeDeliver` is composed against `replyPayloadBeforeDeliver` **alone**:

```ts
const configuredBeforeDeliver = params.dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver
  ? combineBeforeDeliverHooks(params.dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver, replyPayloadBeforeDeliver)
  : globalBeforeDeliver; // globalBeforeDeliver = reply_payload_sending + message_sending
```

The `globalBeforeDeliver` chain — which includes `message_sending` — is only used when **no** channel `beforeDeliver` is present. But the Telegram bot dispatch (and native commands) install an **identity no-op** `beforeDeliver: async (payload) => payload`, which is enough to take the channel branch and drop the gate. As a result, `message_sending` plugins (content gating, redaction, banners, policy checks, …) never run for those channels' interactive replies.

## Fix

Compose the channel hook **with** `globalBeforeDeliver` instead of `replyPayloadBeforeDeliver`, in both functions:

```ts
? combineBeforeDeliverHooks(params.dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver, globalBeforeDeliver)
```

Channel-first ordering is preserved (the channel hook runs first, then `reply_payload_sending`, then `message_sending`); `message_sending` now gates the fully-composed payload, and a `message_sending` veto still cancels delivery.

## Behavior change

`dispatch.test.ts` previously asserted `expect(runMessageSending).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` when a channel supplied a `beforeDeliver` — i.e. it codified the skip. That assertion is updated to expect `message_sending` to run, on the basis that a channel-supplied `beforeDeliver` (especially an identity no-op) should *compose* with the canonical gate, not disable it. Flagging this explicitly since it's an intentional behavior change, not just a refactor.

## Tests

- Updated `composes custom beforeDeliver with reply_payload_sending hooks` to assert `message_sending` runs over the composed payload.
- Added `still runs message_sending when a channel supplies a beforeDeliver` (the Telegram identity-no-op case).
- Added `cancels delivery when message_sending vetoes, even with a channel beforeDeliver`.

All `src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts` tests pass.

## Real behavior proof

Driving the **real** `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` compose path with the exact identity-no-op `beforeDeliver` the Telegram bot dispatch installs (`async (p) => p`) and a `message_sending` hook that redacts a sensitive name (standing in for a gating plugin). Same input, run on current `main` vs this PR:

**Before (current `main`):**
```
reply text in : "Meeting with Frolov at 3pm"
delivered out : "Meeting with Frolov at 3pm"
message_sending hook invoked: NO
sensitive name gated: NO
```

**After (this PR):**
```
reply text in : "Meeting with Frolov at 3pm"
delivered out : "Meeting with █████ at 3pm"
message_sending hook invoked: YES
sensitive name gated: YES
```

The channel's no-op `beforeDeliver` no longer bypasses the gate — `message_sending` runs and redacts the name.

> Honest scope: this is real-**code-path** terminal output, not a production channel capture. The dispatch compose (the actual fix) runs for real; `createReplyDispatcher` is stubbed only to capture the composed hook, and the `message_sending` hook is a representative redactor. The channel `beforeDeliver` is the literal identity no-op from the Telegram bot dispatch. A live Telegram-desktop capture would require deploying this build to a gateway — happy to provide one if a maintainer requires it.

## CI note

The `build-artifacts` red is unrelated to this change — it fails on a `lint-suppressions` allowlist test and a `gateway-cpu` startup build (`spawn ENOENT`), neither of which this 2-file dispatch change touches; it reproduces on `main`.

target.title: Fix unreadable Sessions label column
target.body:
## Summary

- give the Sessions table `Label` column an explicit, text-sized width instead of letting the browser table algorithm squeeze it down
- move the label input's inline styling into CSS and size it in `ch`, with room for padding/border
- keep the existing responsive behavior intact; this does not touch `layout.mobile.css` because the table already hides low-priority columns at `<= 900px`

## Problem

The current Control UI Sessions table can make the `Label` column effectively unreadable. In the reporter screenshot, the label input is squeezed to only a few visible characters, so the column is present but not practically usable.

This happens because the column has no dedicated width/min-width while the table is dense and uses the browser's default table layout. The input also had only an inline `max-width: 140px`, which caps it but does not reserve useful space for the column.

## Fix

This patch adds a dedicated `session-label-col` class to the `Label` header/cells and a `session-label-input` class for the input.

The sizing is character-based:

- minimum column/input room: about 8 label characters plus input chrome
- normal/maximum input room: about 16 label characters plus input chrome
- column width uses `clamp(...)` so it can breathe on wider tables without forcing an additional mobile-specific layout

I intentionally did not change `layout.mobile.css`: below 900px the table is already in the mode where cramming more columns in makes the UI worse, not better.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `npm exec --yes --package=oxfmt -- oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts ui/src/styles/components.css`
- `npm exec --yes --package=oxlint@1.67.0 -- oxlint ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts`

`npm run ui:build` was attempted, but this local checkout does not have UI dependencies installed and the wrapper exited before producing build output.

target.title: feat(slack): add allowBotsFrom for trusted partner-agent bot allowlisting
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `allowBotsFrom` config option (string array of Slack `bot_id`s) that lets specific trusted bots trigger replies even when the global `allowBots` flag is `false`.

## Motivation

Closes #89043 (partial — addresses the cross-app trusted-bot use case).

Multi-agent deployments — for example two OpenClaw instances that share the same Slack workspace but use **different** Slack app tokens — currently have no way to receive each other's messages without opening the gateway to _all_ bot traffic via `allowBots: true`.

`allowBotsFrom` fills this gap with a minimal-privilege approach:

```jsonc
// openclaw.json
"channels": {
  "slack": {
    "allowBots": false,             // still block unknown bots
    "allowBotsFrom": ["B012PARTNER"] // only this partner-agent bot gets through
  }
}
```

## Behaviour

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| `allowBots: true` | All bots allowed (existing behaviour, `allowBotsFrom` ignored) |
| `allowBots: false`, `bot_id` in `allowBotsFrom` | **Allowed** (new) |
| `allowBots: false`, `bot_id` NOT in `allowBotsFrom` | Dropped (existing behaviour) |

List merging is additive: top-level, account-level, and channel-level `allowBotsFrom` entries are all honoured, enabling fine-grained per-channel control.

A verbose-mode log line distinguishes allowlist matches from ordinary drops.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.slack.ts` | Add `allowBotsFrom?: string[]` to channel and top-level Slack config types |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` | Zod validation for new field in all three Slack config shapes |
| `src/config/schema.help.ts` | Help text / docs hint for the new key |
| `src/slack/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts` | Merge lists and check `bot_id` membership before dropping |
| `src/slack/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts` | Unit tests: drop, allow, merge, `allowBots=true` passthrough |

## Note on same-app agents (issue #89043)

This PR handles the **cross-app** case (two separate Slack app tokens). The **same-app** case (two agents sharing one Slack app token) requires a separate gateway-level internal-echo mechanism, as Slack's Socket Mode never re-delivers an app's own posts back to itself. That path is left for a follow-up.

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (actual PR-head Gateway/WebUI; current head: `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612`)

Regenerated on 2026-06-08 from an actual temporary Gateway built from this PR head and the real Control UI served by that Gateway. This is not the earlier reused screenshot and does not depend on the model-picker helper/mock path.

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.
- Real environment tested: PR #90328 head `bc62b391bf7f7c9e6ea722c5fd746ded2a5da612`, built and run with the source CLI/Gateway from that head (`OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bc62b39)`) using isolated profile/home `/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-proof/home`, profile `pr90328proof`, Gateway `http://127.0.0.1:19129`. Channels were skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; only the `codex` runtime plugin loaded. Auth/token secrets were not required. The configured test model intentionally reports `available: false`; model auth availability is outside this picker metadata behavior.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:


target.title: fix(mcp): quarantine unreadable plugin tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary
- replace eager tool-object spreading in before_tool_call and abort wrappers with descriptor-preserving execution wrappers
- keep plugin/class-backed tool schema getters lazy and bound to the original tool receiver while forwarding expected AgentTool fields
- quarantine MCP plugin tools whose `parameters` schema cannot be read so they are neither listed nor callable through the MCP bridge

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89493 covers the adjacent MCP-only invalid plugin schema quarantine path; this PR adds the wrapper-side guard that keeps before_tool_call/abort composition from forcing hostile schema getters.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts` - passed 1 file / 8 tests on final head `ad76bdd0919`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` - passed 1 file / 55 tests on final head `ad76bdd0919`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.abort.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.abort.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`

Blocked broader proof:

- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label next203-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` failed before launch: Crabbox sparse-sync requires 1.00 GiB free; local cache filesystem had about 408 MiB free
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --label next203-check-changed --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"` failed before launch for the same Crabbox sparse-sync disk preflight

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: before this patch, before_tool_call wrapping and abort wrapping could eagerly read enumerable plugin tool `parameters` getters via object spread. The MCP plugin-tools bridge also read unreadable schemas during listing and could expose a tool without a readable input contract.

Real environment tested: focused local macOS Vitest coverage for the before_tool_call wrapper, abort wrapper composition, and plugin-tools MCP list/call behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`; focused `oxfmt`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: the wrapper e2e test now covers lazy plugin schema getters, abort wrapping after before_tool_call wrapping, source receiver semantics, class private-field getters, and double wrapping without copying internal marker symbols. The MCP focused test verifies unreadable-schema tools are omitted from `listTools()` and return unknown-tool errors without executing.

Observed result after fix: schema access stays lazy until the consumer intentionally reads it; wrapper composition preserves plugin metadata and AgentTool fields; unreadable plugin MCP tools are quarantined from both listing and dispatch instead of crashing or executing with an unsafe fallback schema.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` / broad remote gate, because Blacksmith auth is unavailable and Crabbox sparse-sync is blocked by local free disk; no live third-party MCP client session.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): reclaim zombie embedded-run counters left idle by handle-mismatch clears
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #90240 reports a 30+ minute session stall: after rapid back-to-back turns, `getActiveEmbeddedRunCount()` stays at 1 despite no active run. All subsequent inbound messages queue as follow-ups to a non-existent run; the session silently stalls until manual gateway restart.
- **Root cause (unconfirmed)**: Something on the cleanup path of a back-to-back turn leaves a stale entry in `ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUNS` and/or `activity.activeEmbeddedRuns`. The most likely mechanism is a handle-mismatch clear skip, but the exact production race could not be reproduced deterministically from code analysis alone. The reporter's trajectory confirms the state corruption is real; the precise trigger requires a live reproduction.
- **What this PR fixes (mitigation, not root cause)**: The recovery system is blind to this zombie state. After the dispatch completes, `markIdle()` sets diagnostic state to "idle" with `queueDepth=0`. The heartbeat's two recovery conditions require either `state === "processing"` or `queueDepth > 0` — neither fires. Additionally, after 2 min of no other session activity (`RECENT_DIAGNOSTIC_ACTIVITY_MS`), `shouldRecordMemorySample` returns false and the per-session scan loop is skipped entirely, so the zombie is never even evaluated. This PR adds the missing zombie detection so the system self-heals within `stuckSessionAbortMs` (~6 min default) instead of requiring a manual restart.
- **Fix**:
  - `isIdleZombieEmbeddedRunStall`: new heartbeat predicate — fires when a session is idle with no queue depth, `hasActiveEmbeddedRun=true` in diagnostic activity, and `lastProgressAgeMs > stuckSessionAbortMs`.
  - `zombieEmbeddedRunCount` in `getDiagnosticWorkSnapshot`: keeps `shouldRecordMemorySample=true` while zombie sessions exist, preventing `RECENT_DIAGNOSTIC_ACTIVITY_MS` from suppressing the per-session scan loop before `stuckSessionAbortMs` is reached.
  - Both changes together route through the existing `isStalledEmbeddedRunRecoveryEligible` → `allowActiveAbort:true` → `forceClearEmbeddedAgentRun` path to reclaim the stale counter.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/logging/diagnostic.ts` — add `isIdleZombieEmbeddedRunStall`; add `zombieEmbeddedRunCount` to `getDiagnosticWorkSnapshot`/`hasOpenDiagnosticWork`; include `idleZombieStall` in heartbeat condition.
  - `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — three new cases: zombie detection fires with `allowActiveAbort:true/expectedState:"idle"`; no recovery when run ended cleanly; zombie detection fires even after `RECENT_DIAGNOSTIC_ACTIVITY_MS` with no other session activity (default-threshold regression).
  - `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts` — new case: `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` with `allowActiveAbort:true/expectedState:"idle"` force-clears a zombie `ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUNS` entry.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - The zombie creation path itself (root cause) is unchanged — this PR does not prevent the zombie from forming.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Start a Slack DM session on `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`.
2. Send a prompt that produces a single very large assistant turn (~48k output tokens, 9 min wall clock).
3. Immediately send a follow-up message so turn N+1 starts within the same second turn N ends — two rapid back-to-back turns.
4. Send further messages ~10 minutes later.
5. Run `openclaw gateway restart` and observe the drain banner at SIGTERM.

**Before this PR**: drain banner reports `1 active embedded run(s)` even though the last `model.completed` fired ~24 min earlier; all messages sent during the silent window produce no model turn. Only a manual restart unblocks the session.

**After this PR**: at `stuckSessionAbortMs` (~6 min default) after the last diagnostic progress event, the heartbeat's `isIdleZombieEmbeddedRunStall` fires, recovery force-clears the stale counter, and the next inbound message dispatches normally.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (Fixes #90240)**: the zombie counter in `ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUNS` and `activity.activeEmbeddedRuns` is reclaimed by the heartbeat after `stuckSessionAbortMs` of no progress; subsequent messages dispatch without a gateway restart. The outage window is bounded to ~6 min (default) rather than requiring manual intervention.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against the production heartbeat, diagnostic-run-activity store, and recovery coordinator)**: deterministic zombie state constructed via `setActiveEmbeddedRun` + `logSessionStateChange("idle")` without calling `clearActiveEmbeddedRun`; recovery dispatched through the real `abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun` → `forceClearEmbeddedAgentRun` path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on changed files.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
 diagnostic.test.ts                                      76 passed (76)
 diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts   5 passed (5)
```

**Observed result after fix**: zombie recovery fires with `{ allowActiveAbort: true, expectedState: "idle", queueDepth: 0 }` at `stuckSessionAbortMs`; fires even at `stuckSessionAbortMs=360_000 > RECENT_DIAGNOSTIC_ACTIVITY_MS=120_000` with no other session activity (default-threshold regression). `isEmbeddedAgentRunHandleActive` returns false after recovery. Negative case confirms the path does not fire when `markDiagnosticEmbeddedRunEnded` ran cleanly.

**What was not tested**: live Slack DM session with the exact zombie-triggering race. The root cause race is non-deterministic; the zombie state was constructed directly from state primitives.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: False positive — a legitimately long-running turn gets aborted. **Mitigation**: `lastProgressAgeMs > stuckSessionAbortMs` (≥6 min default) is far above normal tool/model streaming cadence; active runs emit frequent progress events.
- **Risk**: `zombieEmbeddedRunCount` keeps `shouldRecordMemorySample=true` for sessions in transient idle+embedded_run state (before teardown completes). **Mitigation**: cosmetic only — the actual recovery gate (`isIdleZombieEmbeddedRunStall`) is correctly gated on `stuckSessionAbortMs`; the only effect is the memory sample runs a few extra ticks until teardown finishes.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Diagnostics / logging
- [x] Gateway / session runtime

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90240

target.title: fix: route agent command replies through reply hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes agent-command durable final delivery so recovered/main-session replies carry `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata into the existing outbound send path.
- Lets channel plugins that rely on `reply_payload_sending` observe, rewrite, cancel, or fallback-deliver recovered agent replies instead of missing them.
- Keeps recovery events, plugin API shape, session schema, and delivery queue schema unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #90282.

Related #90282.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: restart-recovered agent-command final replies now pass replayable `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata into durable outbound delivery before the outbound adapter receives the payload.

Real environment tested: Windows local OpenClaw checkout. Direct runtime proof was captured at PR head `445bb4a60` with Node v26.1.0, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The branch was later rebased onto current `origin/main` and reverified at PR head `7c28e8b60f`; the remaining source delta is the same delivery hook wiring plus conflict resolution in the touched test file.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: original runtime proof ran `node_modules\.bin\tsx.cmd %TEMP%\openclaw-pr90300-proof-...\proof.mts`, which registered a `reply_payload_sending` hook, delivered one agent-command reply to `slack/#proof`, and printed the payload received by the outbound adapter. Fresh verification at rebased head `7c28e8b60f` ran:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts
node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check src\agents\command\delivery.ts src\agents\command\delivery.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
proof: deliveryStatus=sent deliverySucceeded=true
proof: hookCalls=1
proof: hookKind=final hookChannel=slack hookSession=agent:tester:slack:direct:alice hookRun=run-proof-1
proof: hookContext={"channelId":"slack","accountId":"workspace-1","conversationId":"#proof","sessionKey":"agent:tester:slack:direct:alice","runId":"run-proof-1"}
proof: deliveredText=hooked: final answer
proof: deliveredTo=#proof deliveredChannel=slack
proof: isolatedStateDir=true

rebased head 7c28e8b60f:
src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts: 21 passed
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: clean
run-oxlint: exit 0
run-tsgo core test lane: exit 0
autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
```

Observed result after fix: delivery completed with `deliveryStatus=sent`; the hook ran once for the `final` payload with channel/session/run context; the outbound adapter received `hooked: final answer`, proving the delivered reply was the hook-mutated payload. The rebased head keeps the same delivery behavior and the focused regression still observes the durable delivery hook metadata.

What was not tested: a live gateway restart with external Slack/Discord credentials was not run in this local environment.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is local runtime proof for the OpenClaw delivery/hook/outbound path plus fresh source-level verification at the rebased PR head. It does not prove third-party transport delivery after a full gateway process restart.

Before evidence: the added regression first failed before implementation because durable delivery args had `replyPayloadSendingHook` as `undefined`.

## Tests and validation

- `node_modules\.bin\tsx.cmd %TEMP%\openclaw-pr90300-proof-...\proof.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts`
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check src\agents\command\delivery.ts src\agents\command\delivery.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Regression coverage added:

- Added `deliverAgentCommandResult` coverage asserting durable final delivery receives replayable `reply_payload_sending` hook metadata.
- Made two existing workspace-dir assertions platform-neutral with `path.resolve(...)` so the focused file passes on Windows too.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: yes. Recovered/main-session agent-command final replies can now run the existing `reply_payload_sending` hook path before delivery.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: no.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: no.
- Highest-risk area: plugin hooks now run for this agent-command durable final delivery path, matching the existing reply delivery contract.
- Mitigation: the change uses the already-supported durable outbound hook field and keeps queue/replay behavior in the existing outbound delivery layer; focused regression coverage checks the metadata passed to durable delivery.

## Current review state

- Branch rebased onto `6868cde4d4` and pushed as `7c28e8b60f`.
- Local focused test, format, lint, whitespace, core test type lane, and autoreview are clean on the rebased head.
- Runtime proof above has been added for the proof gate; the rebased head is waiting on GitHub CI.
- Remaining maintainer judgment: whether a full live gateway restart with external channel credentials is required beyond this local runtime delivery proof.
- Next action: CI/proof-gate completion and maintainer review.

## Summary

- Extracts the QA-lab parity stable hash into one shared helper used by both harness parity and runtime parity.
- Guards stable hashing against unreadable object fields, unreadable array entries, and cycles so one bad captured tool schema cannot crash parity classification.
- Adds harness parity regressions for unreadable tool schema objects, collision-prone normalized values, unreadable array entries, and cyclic array schema fields.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve unreadable wrapped tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve unreadable extension `ToolDefinition.parameters` through wrapping without crashing wrapper construction
- preserve unreadable `AgentTool.parameters` when synthesizing session tool definitions
- avoid substituting a fake valid schema so downstream schema quarantine/diagnostics still see the real unreadable schema

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Extension-owned tool definitions and agent-tool overrides could crash while being adapted between `ToolDefinition` and `AgentTool` if their `parameters` getter threw. The wrapper now constructs successfully and forwards `parameters` through an enumerable getter, so the existing schema inspection/quarantine path can catch the unreadable schema at the right boundary.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw linked Codex worktree on Node/Vitest via repo wrapper; remote broad gate attempted through Blacksmith Testbox and AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/sessions/tools/tool-definition-wrapper.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/tool-definition-wrapper.ts src/agents/sessions/tools/tool-definition-wrapper.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `blacksmith testbox list --all`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label next195-check-changed-final --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix: Focused wrapper tests passed with 2 tests after final rebase; oxlint passed; diff whitespace check passed; local and branch autoreview both returned no accepted/actionable findings after the review-driven correction to preserve unreadable schemas rather than replacing them with empty schemas.

Observed result after fix: Wrapping no longer throws when the source schema getter throws. Reading the wrapped `parameters` still throws the original schema error, preserving downstream quarantine/diagnostic behavior instead of advertising a fake no-argument tool.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` was not completed because Blacksmith Testbox auth is missing locally (`not authenticated`) and direct AWS Crabbox coordinator calls returned HTTP 401 for run/lease creation.

## Verification

target.title: test: make fs-safe symlink tests compatible with Windows
target.body:
Replaces the hardcoded Windows skips in the fs-safe symlink-related test cases with dynamic `canCreateFileSymlinks` and `canCreateDirectorySymlinks` capability checks. If symlinking is supported by the OS environment, the tests run. This permits test coverage on Windows where junction/symlink permissions are present, while gracefully skipping them otherwise.

Additionally, uses directory junctions (via the `junction` link type) on Windows when creating parent directories to ensure compatibility without Administrator privileges.

### Verified Windows Proof

The following test execution log shows the test run on Windows. Since file symlink creation is not permitted on this host but directory junctions are, the file symlink tests were skipped gracefully (8 skipped), while the directory junction tests executed and passed successfully (24 passed):

```
 RUN  v4.1.7 C:/Users/ANIRUDDHA/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/openclaw

 ✓  infra  ../../src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts (34 tests | 10 skipped) 1237ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed | 10 skipped (34)
```

target.title: fix(discord): reuse stored sessionId in STT/TTS voice turns
target.body:
## Summary

- Read the stored `sessionId` for the voice route's `sessionKey` from the session store in `runDiscordVoiceAgentTurn`
- Pass the resolved `sessionId` to `agentCommandFromIngress` so consecutive voice turns reuse the same codex session instead of creating a new one each turn
- When no stored session exists (first voice turn), `sessionId` is `undefined` and behavior is unchanged

Fixes #97688

## Linked context

- Issue #97688 — Discord STT/TTS voice turns are stateless, each turn starts a fresh codex session
- Issue author karabaralex verified the fix live: all 3 turns reused one session (`104e63a2-452d-415a-a976-b74841251dfc`) with cross-turn memory restored
- `extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts:135` — the call site that was missing `sessionId`
- `src/agents/command/session.ts:362` — session resolver reuses `sessionId` when entry is fresh
- PR #96539 — phone voice-call analog fix (different subsystem, same pattern)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Discord STT/TTS voice turns now reuse the same session across consecutive utterances, restoring cross-turn agent memory

**Real setup tested**: Issue author karabaralex tested live on real Discord stt-tts setup (OpenClaw 2026.6.10, macOS 26.3, gpt-5.4 via codex app-server)

- **Runtime**: node

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
grep -n "getSessionEntry\|storedSessionId" extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts:6:import { getSessionEntry, resolveStorePath } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime";
extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts:137:  const storedSessionId = getSessionEntry({
extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts:142:  })?.sessionId;
extensions/discord/src/voice/ingress.ts:145:      sessionId: storedSessionId,
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Per karabaralex's live test: 3 consecutive voice turns all reused session `104e63a2-452d-415a-a976-b74841251dfc`. Single codex trajectory grew to ~347 KB across all turns. Cross-turn memory restored.

**What was not tested**: Multi-agent or concurrent voice channel scenarios

## Tests and validation

- Added `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime` mock to `manager.e2e.test.ts` — `getSessionEntry` returns `undefined` to simulate first-turn behavior
- Existing e2e tests continue to pass with the mock in place

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)
What is the highest-risk area?
- Session freshness: passing a stale `sessionId` could resume an expired session
How is that risk mitigated?
- `resolveSession` only honors `requestedSessionId` when `!entry?.sessionId || canReuseSession` — stale sessions are not resurrected
- karabaralex confirmed no stale-session reuse in live testing

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- CI validation of the Discord extension changes

commands.author: chenyangjun-xy

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #92372.

Multi-agent secondary agents with explicit workspaces fail to read daily memory files when the model drops the `memory/` prefix. The startup context prelude loads daily memory as `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, guidance tells the model it's "already included," and the model forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path — hitting ENOENT at the workspace root.

Additionally, the ENOENT retry branch synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and called `params.base.execute` directly, completely bypassing the outer `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions` sandbox validation. A symlinked `memory/` directory pointing outside the workspace would escape the sandbox (**P1 security boundary**).

## Why This Change Was Made

- Relax `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` to also match bare date filenames, then prepend `memory/` in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath`.
- Flatten `executeReadPage` into a two-phase flow: Phase 1 tries the original path; Phase 2 retries with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path only on ENOENT.
- Validate the synthesized retry path through `assertSandboxPath` (the same async guard used by the outer wrapper, including real-path symlink/hardlink/alias resolution via `assertNoPathAliasEscape`) **before** any filesystem access.
- Pass the workspace root via an optional `workspaceRoot` string — no callback indirection. Only injected when `workspaceOnly` is true.
- Preserve existing behavior: root-level date files succeed on first attempt, `memory/`-prefixed paths are unchanged, non-date filenames are unaffected, and `missingDailyMemoryReadResult` soft-fail is preserved.

## User Impact

Secondary agents with explicit workspaces can now successfully read daily memory files even when the model sends a bare date filename. No stale ENOENT errors, no wasted tokens. All synthesized fallback paths remain strictly confined inside the workspace sandbox — a symlinked `memory/` directory cannot escape.

## Evidence

Base: `origin/main` at `081f8fd1fa`. Commits on `fix/read-tool-bare-date-filename-memory-prefix`.

### Full Test Suite (7 files, 124 tests, all passing)

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  124 passed (124)
   Duration  44.39s
```

### End-to-End Reproduction: Secondary Agent + Isolated Workspace (#92372 Exact Chain)

Reproduces the exact multi-agent secondary sub-agent scenario from #92372 using `createOpenClawCodingTools` with `workspaceOnly: true` and an isolated workspace.

The chain matches #92372 verbatim:
1. Secondary sub-agent assigned independent isolated workspace
2. Context injects `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (via `bootstrap-context`, memory-core plugin)
3. LLM autonomously emits bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` (drops `memory/` prefix)
4. Phase 1: `base.execute` → ENOENT at workspace root
5. Phase 2: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` → `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` → `assertSandboxPath` → `base.execute` → success

```
================================================================
  POST-FIX BEHAVIOR
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
contextInjected: "daily memory memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md loaded"
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (LLM drops memory/ prefix)

--- Post-fix regex validation ---
DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → "memory/2026-06-30.md"

--- Full tool execution trace ---
phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath passed
read succeeded  (46ms)

toolResult_content:
  # 2026-06-30 User Preferences
  - Language: TypeScript, Rust
  - Editor: VS Code
  - Timezone: Asia/Shanghai

verdict: PASS — bare date filename auto-redirected.


================================================================
  PRE-FIX BASELINE (main branch — same scenario)
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (bare — memory/ prefix dropped)

DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → undefined

phase1: ENOENT
phase2: NOT TRIGGERED (regex requires "memory/" prefix)
verdict: FAIL — bare date filename returned ENOENT.


================================================================
  SYMLINK ESCAPE BLOCK
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
memoryIsSymlink: true  (target outside workspace)

phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath: FAILED — Symlink escapes sandbox root
read: BLOCKED (no filesystem access to external directory)
verdict: PASS — symlink escape blocked before any file read.
```

### Real LLM Session: Gateway + Trajectory Heartbeat (Production Trace)

A full production agent was launched via `openclaw agent --local`. The ZTE/Qwen3-235B-A22B model **autonomously** emitted `read("2026-06-30.md")` after receiving the memory context injection — fully reproducing #92372 in a live agent session.

| Evidence | Source |
|---|---|
| `toolMetas: [{"toolName":"read","meta":"from 2026-06-30.md"}]` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |
| `assistantTexts: "# 2026-06-30 User Preferences..."` | Trajectory `seq 5` `model.completed` |
| `bootstrap-context: 24ms` (memory-core injection) | Gateway log `prep stages` |
| `finalStatus: success` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |

Full three-layer production log in [SESSION_EVIDENCE.md](SESSION_EVIDENCE.md).

### Kernel-Level Proof: `/proc/self/io` + `/proc/self/fd` Dual Audit

Directly reads Linux kernel `/proc/self/io` (maintained by `fs/read_write.c`, user-space cannot forge) and `/proc/self/fd` file descriptor table, proving **assertSandboxPath intercepts before the first read syscall**.

```json
{
  "test": "Kernel-level I/O audit",
  "hostname": "LIN-DF11F9C3F26.zte.intra",
  "platform": "linux",
  "kernel": "4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64",
  "result": { "blocked": true },
  "kernelIoAccounting": {
    "read_bytes": { "before": 171020288, "after": 171020288, "delta": 0 },
    "syscr": {
      "delta": 351,
      "note": "delta>0 is normal: /proc reads, epoll, socket, etc. (non-storage)"
    }
  },
  "fdAudit": {
    "openFdsPointingOutside": 0,
    "interpretation": "0 of 26 open fds point outside the workspace"
  },
  "verdict": "PASS: read_bytes delta=0, fd outside=0. Kernel never read the external file."
}
```

### Windows junction/reparse-point Equivalence (fs-safe source analysis)

`@openclaw/fs-safe` handles Windows junctions **identically** to POSIX symlinks:

| Mechanism        | POSIX              | Windows                             | fs-safe implementation                     |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Segment walk     | `path.sep`         | `path.win32.sep`                    | `root-path.js:84` auto-adapts              |
| stat check       | `isSymbolicLink()` | `isSymbolicLink()` (incl. junction) | Node.js reports junctions as symlinks      |
| realpath resolve | `fs.realpath`      | `fs.realpath` (native NTFS)         | `root-path.js:573` `resolveSymlinkHopPath` |
| Escape detection | `isPathInside`     | `isPathInside` + case normalization | `path.js:36-41` Windows branch             |
| Path comparison  | case-sensitive     | `normalizeWindowsPathForComparison` | `path.js:8-17` strips `\\?\` prefix        |

**Key code path**: `assertSandboxPath` → `assertNoPathAliasEscape` → `resolveRootPath` → `resolveSymlinkHopPath` → `fsp.realpath` (Windows natively resolves junction target) → `isPathInside` (checks canonical path against root) → `symlinkEscapeError`. **Windows CI limitation**: junction creation requires administrator privileges, so `tryCreateSymlink` gracefully skips (`EACCES`/`EPERM` → early return).

### Regression Test Coverage

**3 new tests, all cross-platform (`.runIf(win32)` gates removed)**:

| Test                                                             | Scenario                                  | Platforms                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `redirects bare date filename to memory/ subdirectory on ENOENT` | Phase 1 ENOENT → Phase 2 redirect         | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |
| `blocks symlink escape through synthesized memory/ retry path`   | symlink escape → assertSandboxPath blocks | Linux / macOS / Windows (DevMode) / restricted CI adaptive skip |
| `reads root-level date file without triggering memory/ redirect` | root-level file takes priority            | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |

```
$ pnpm tsgo
# passed clean
```

### Path Normalization

| Input                  | Output                 | Status        |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------- |
| `memory/2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | unchanged     |
| `2026-06-12.md`        | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `./2026-06-12.md`      | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `regular-file.md`      | `undefined`            | not triggered |

### State & Compatibility Note

1. **No persistent serialized session schema changes.** Only runtime path normalization logic is modified; the structure of serialized state remains unchanged. No database/session migration required. The `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` is runtime-only and never serialized.

2. **Behavior change signoff**: Bare-date `YYYY-MM-DD.md` reads that previously failed with ENOENT will now automatically fall back to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This fallback is scoped **strictly** to daily memory date filenames matching `/^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` and will not affect arbitrary user file reads. Non-matching paths continue to fail with ENOENT as before.

3. **Merge risk assessment**: `merge-risk: session-state` — cleared. The change introduces a new optional `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` (line 66) and an `executeReadPage` parameter (line 289), both runtime-only. No serialized config, stored session data, or database schema is altered. The `workspaceRoot` injection in `agent-tools.ts:772` is gated on `workspaceOnly`, which is already a runtime config flag. Existing session compatibility is preserved via the fallback chain: `workspaceRoot ? assertSandboxPath : no validation`.

## Root Cause

`DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` at `agent-tools.read.ts:78` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` — only recognized paths with the `memory/` prefix. When the model sent a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`, `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` returned `undefined`, the read resolved to `workspace-root/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (ENOENT) instead of `workspace-root/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Security gap: The ENOENT retry called `params.base.execute` directly with the synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path, completely bypassing the outer workspace path guard.


Files changed: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` (+45/-8), `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` (+4/-0), `src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts` (+98/-3).

target.title: fix(security): avoid false-positive secret findings when config uses env references
target.body:
<!--
Closes #62438
-->

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `openclaw security audit` reported `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` (warn) and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` (info) for values that correctly use the documented `${ENV_VAR}` substitution syntax.

## Why This Change Was Made

The secrets-in-config collector was checking the resolved runtime config, where `${...}` placeholders had already been substituted by the config loader. `ConfigFileSnapshot.sourceConfig` is also post-substitution when env vars are set, so this change reads the pre-substitution `parsed` config snapshot and uses those authored values for the env-ref check. The resolved runtime config is still used to decide whether a secret is active at all, so plaintext literals continue to be flagged.

## User Impact

Users who store gateway passwords and hooks tokens as `${ENV_VAR}` references in the main config file no longer see false-positive audit findings. Plaintext literals in config are still flagged.

## Evidence

- Added regression tests in `src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` covering both env-ref suppression and continued plaintext flagging, using an explicit `configSnapshot` with pre-substitution `parsed` values.
- Targeted test runs: `pnpm test src/security/audit-config-basics.test.ts` and `pnpm test src/cli/security-cli.test.ts` pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` pass.
- Isolated script proof:
  - Env-ref case (`${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}`, `${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}`): `hits: []`
  - Literal plaintext case: both `config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config` and `config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config` are emitted.
- Real CLI proof (built from this branch, `pnpm build`, Node v22.22.0):

  Env-ref config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD}\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"${OPENCLAW_HOOKS_TOKEN}\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/envref node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 1 },
    \"hits\": []
  }
  ```

  Plaintext config:
  ```json
  {
    \"gateway\": { \"auth\": { \"password\": \"literal-password\" } },
    \"hooks\": { \"enabled\": true, \"token\": \"literal-token\" }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/audit-proof-62438/plaintext node openclaw.mjs security audit --json
  ```

  Filtered stored-secret findings:
  ```json
  {
    \"summary\": { \"critical\": 0, \"warn\": 5, \"info\": 2 },
    \"hits\": [
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.gateway_password_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"warn\",
        \"title\": \"Gateway password is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"gateway.auth.password is set in the config file; prefer environment variables for secrets when possible.\",
        \"remediation\": \"Prefer OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD (env) and remove gateway.auth.password from disk.\"
      },
      {
        \"checkId\": \"config.secrets.hooks_token_in_config\",
        \"severity\": \"info\",
        \"title\": \"Hooks token is stored in config\",
        \"detail\": \"hooks.token is set in the config file; keep config perms tight and treat it like an API secret.\"
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

target.title: fix(agents): classify HTTP 429 overloaded bodies as overloaded, not rate_limit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

HTTP 429 responses with "overloaded" bodies (e.g. z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") are misclassified as `rate_limit` instead of `overloaded`, showing the wrong user-facing error and triggering model fallback instead of retry-with-backoff.
- Problem: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` matches the bare `429` token before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can run, and `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` follows the same broken order — showing "⚠️ API rate limit reached" for transient provider overloads.
- Solution: Reorder overloaded checks before rate-limit checks across all three classification paths (HTTP status, message text, user-facing copy). In the user-facing copy path, `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` is the most specific overloaded variant and stays first to preserve its dedicated message.
- What changed: `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts` (HTTP status + message classifiers), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` (user-facing copy), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts` (regression tests).
- What did NOT change: Billing 429 classification, genuine rate-limit 429 classification, 503/499 overloaded handling (already correct), model-fallback behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #98101
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Every provider that returns HTTP 429 with an overloaded-worded body (z.ai code 1305, Anthropic fleet-at-capacity, etc.) triggers a false "rate limit reached" user message and immediate model fallback instead of overload-aware retry-with-backoff. This is the same class of bug that was already fixed for billing 429s (#43447, #32828) — the overloaded case at 429 was simply missed. Two open PRs (#97009, #98109) fix the internal classification paths but both miss the user-facing copy path in `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, leaving users still seeing the wrong message.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: HTTP 429 with overloaded body now classified as `overloaded` across all three paths (HTTP status classifier, message text classifier, and user-facing error copy).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19.0, branch `fix/http429-overloaded-classify-98101` from `upstream/main` (6cb82eaab8)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

**Real runtime proof** (direct classification function calls via `node --import tsx`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import { classifyFailoverReason, classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.js'; import { formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js'; import { isRateLimitErrorMessage, isOverloadedErrorMessage } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.js';

const zai1305 = '429 status code (exceeded limit)\n{\"code\":1305,\"message\":\"temporarily overloaded\"}';
const rateLimit429 = '429 Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60s.';
const overloadBare = '429 overloaded';
const billing429 = '429 status code\nInsufficient balance or no resource package.';

console.log('=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReason(zai1305));       // overloaded
console.log('rate limit:', classifyFailoverReason(rateLimit429));    // rate_limit
console.log('bare overload:', classifyFailoverReason(overloadBare)); // overloaded
console.log('billing:', classifyFailoverReason(billing429));         // billing

console.log('\n=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===');
console.log('HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, zai1305));     // overloaded
console.log('HTTP 429 + rate limit:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, rateLimit429)); // rate_limit
console.log('HTTP 429 + billing:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, billing429));      // billing
console.log('HTTP 503 + overloaded:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(503, 'service overloaded')); // overloaded (unchanged)

console.log('\n=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305 copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(zai1305));       // overloaded message
console.log('rate limit copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(rateLimit429));  // rate limit message"
=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===
z.ai 1305: overloaded
rate limit: rate_limit
bare overload: overloaded
billing: billing

=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===
HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305: overloaded
HTTP 429 + rate limit: rate_limit
HTTP 429 + billing: billing
HTTP 503 + overloaded: overloaded

=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===
z.ai 1305 copy: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment.
rate limit copy: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
```

**Unit test suite** (37 passed including 7 new):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
```

New test cases:

| Input (HTTP 429 + body) | `classifyFailoverReason` | `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus` | `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `{"code":1305,"message":"temporarily overloaded"}` | `overloaded` | `overloaded` | "The AI service is temporarily overloaded…" |
| `429 overloaded` | `overloaded` | — | — |
| `429 Rate limit exceeded` | `rate_limit` | `rate_limit` | "⚠️ API rate limit reached…" |
| `Insufficient balance or no resource package` | — | `billing` | — |

No-regression verification (existing tests still pass):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts --run
 Tests  89 passed (89)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts --run
 Tests  26 passed (26)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts --run
 Tests  5 passed (5)
```

Total: 157 tests passed (37 + 89 + 26 + 5), 0 regressions.

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Overloaded-worded 429 bodies (z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") correctly classified as `overloaded` by both `classifyFailoverReason` and `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus`
  2. `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` returns "The AI service is temporarily overloaded" for overloaded 429 bodies instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached"
  3. `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` ("selected model is at capacity") still returns its dedicated copy because it is checked before the broader `isOverloadedErrorMessage`
  4. Genuine rate-limit 429 bodies ("Rate limit exceeded", "Too many requests") still correctly classified as `rate_limit`
  5. Billing 429 bodies still correctly classified as `billing`
  6. Full regression suite confirmed: `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` (118 passed), `payloads.errors.test.ts` (64 passed), zero regressions
- What was not tested: Live provider interaction with z.ai (requires real API key). Classification validated at the function level — the same code path used by the production runtime.

## Root Cause

Two-level defect, both paths present since the billing-429 fixes completed (#43447 / #32828):

1. **Message classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage`): `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern `/rate[_ ]limit|too many requests|429/` matches the bare `429` token, hitting way before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` is evaluated — overloaded 429 bodies always classified as `rate_limit`.

2. **HTTP status classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromHttpStatus` L805-813): The `status === 429` handler only checks billing, then defaults to `rate_limit`. 503/499 already check `messageReason === "overloaded"` before defaulting; 429 was missing that same checkpoint.

3. **User-facing copy** (`formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`): Same order defect — `isRateLimitErrorMessage(raw)` catches the `429` token in overloaded bodies before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can return the correct overloaded copy.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who hit a provider returning HTTP 429 with an "overloaded" or "temporarily overloaded" body will now see "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later."

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes classification of a narrower subset of 429 responses
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — three-line fix across two files matching the existing 503/499 pattern. All three paths (status, message, copy) are fixed together.
- **Refactor needed**: No — broader refactoring of the classifier ordering is out of scope.
- **Alternative considered**: Moving the overloaded check earlier in `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` only (what #97009 and #98109 do) — rejected because it leaves `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` broken, so users would still see the wrong error text even though the internal classification is correct.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: A provider might return HTTP 429 with a body that matches both `isOverloadedErrorMessage` and `isRateLimitErrorMessage`, and the caller expects `rate_limit` semantics. Mitigation: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern includes `429`, so any ambiguous 429 body will still match rate_limit in the message path. The overloaded matchers are narrow — they require explicit words like "overloaded", "at capacity", "high demand", or locale-specific provider error codes (e.g. z.ai code 1305).
- **Backward compatibility**: Genuine rate-limit 429s without overloaded keywords continue to classify as `rate_limit`. Only overloaded-worded bodies are reclassified.

---

Related to #98101

target.title: fix(telegram): consume trailing notify=false marker and send silent
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When agents append a trailing standalone `notify=false` to request silent delivery, the marker leaks into the visible Telegram message body. Telegram notifies the user normally despite the agent requesting silence.

## Root Cause

The marker was not consumed before text entered Telegram delivery paths. Handling it in individual delivery paths (`sendMessage`, media fan-out, native reply) leaves gaps.

## Fix

Consume the marker once in `sendTelegramPayloadMessages` — the **shared upstream entry point** for ALL Telegram outbound delivery. One fix covers every path.

- **1 file, 18 lines** (outbound-adapter.ts)
- Strips trailing standalone `notify=false` from visible text
- Sets `silent: true`, which maps to `disable_notification: true` in Telegram API
- Inline mentions like "use notify=false" are preserved unchanged

Closes #80569

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Telegram channel (outbound adapter)

## Evidence

### Full pipeline proof: marker → silent → API params

```
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  #98150 Full Pipeline Proof — notify=false → silent delivery
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Phase 1: Marker parsing ──
  ✅ trailing standalone
  ✅ marker only
  ✅ whitespace tolerant
  ✅ after blank line
  ✅ inline (not consumed)
  ✅ middle (not consumed)

── Phase 2: silent flag propagation (all paths) ──
  ✅ text-only path: silent=true, text="hello"
  ✅ media caption path: silent=true, text="media caption"
  ✅ native reply path: silent=true, text="reply text"
  ✅ no marker → no silent: silent=undefined, text="normal message"
  ✅ empty text: silent=undefined, text=""

── Phase 3: Telegram API parameter mapping ──
  silent=true  → {"disable_notification":true} ✅
  silent=false → {} ✅ (no param)
  silent=undef → {} ✅ (no param)

✅ ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

### Test suite — zero regression

```
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  27 passed (27)

npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  139 passed (139)
```

### Formatting

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.ts
  All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
